title: рҹҺЁ Palette: Consistent semantic colors for textual statuses
body:
рҹ’Ў What: I've updated the `_print_configuration_summary` method in `src/main.py` so that textual status indicators like `Enabled`, `Disabled` and `None` use consistent visual styles (e.g. `Colors.GREEN` and `Colors.GREY`).

рҹҺҜ Why: I found an inconsistency in the configuration summaries where textual indicators were missing appropriate ANSI coloring, hurting scannability.

рҹ“ё Before/After: Before, `Enabled` was just text. Now, it is printed in green with a checkmark.

вҷҝ Accessibility: Using colors and distinct semantic signs improves visual structure and helps users instantly understand the configuration states.
head: palette-ux-improvement
